clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Guide blood pressure management in acute ischemic stroke to optimize outcomes and prevent harm
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Acute ischemic stroke presentation, before thrombolytics/endovascular treatment, post-reperfusion monitoring
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: BP target selection, treatment initiation timing, complication prevention

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for acute ischemic stroke assessment
Measure initial BP and assess hypovolemia/hypotension
Determine IV thrombolytic or endovascular treatment eligibility
Match patient presentation to appropriate COR/LOE pathway
Execute BP lowering or maintenance protocol
Evaluate functional outcomes and complication prevention

validationRules: BP must be measured before treatment decision
Reperfusion status required for post-procedure recommendations

rules: If hypotension/hypovolemia present → COR 1, LOE C-LD: correct to maintain systemic perfusion
If eligible for IV thrombolytics AND elevated BP → COR 1, LOE B-NR: lower BP to SBP <185, DBP <110 before treatment, maintain <180/105 for 24h post
If undergoing endovascular treatment → COR 2a, LOE B-NR: reasonable to maintain BP ≤180/105 during and 24h after
If BP ≥220/120, no thrombolytics/endovascular, no comorbid conditions requiring treatment → COR 2b, LOE C-LD: might be reasonable to lower BP by 15% in first 24h
If BP <220/120, no thrombolytics/endovascular, no comorbid conditions → COR 3 No Benefit, LOE A: initiating/reinitiating treatment in first 48-72h not effective to prevent death/disability
If successful brain reperfusion with endovascular treatment for large vessel occlusion → COR 3 Harm, LOE A: lowering SBP <140 within 24-72h worsens functional outcome

symptomsToAsk: Stroke symptoms (speech difficulty, weakness, vision changes)

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: Emergency BP management, consider IV thrombolytics if within window
escalationLogic.ifNoSymptoms: Monitor closely, consider COR 2b pathway (15% reduction over 24h)

appDisplay.screenType: Stroke alert with BP crisis protocol
appDisplay.behavior: Route to acute stroke management pathway

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Follow COR-specific BP target: Neurology consultation for thrombolytic/endovascular eligibility
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Monitor BP every 15 min during intervention, then hourly for 24h, then per stroke unit protocol
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Check BP before each treatment decision point; re-evaluate at 24h, 48h, and 72h post-stroke
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Alert stroke team if BP exceeds targets or if COR 3 Harm criteria identified (SBP <140 post-reperfusion)
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log BP measurements, COR/LOE applied, intervention performed, and 90-day functional outcome
